name: Food-Where does it Come From Test 01

questions.question: Given below are names of some animals: Goat Human beings Cockroach Eagle Which of the above animals form a pair of omnivores?
questions.question_html:
Given below are names of some animals:

  1. Goat
  2. Human beings
  3. Cockroach
  4. Eagle

Which of the above animals form a pair of omnivores?

questions.question: Given below is a list of edible plants: Banana Pumpkin Lady's finger Brinjal What pair of plants have two or more edible parts?

questions.question: An animal that eats other animals is called as
questions.question_html: An animal that eats other animals is called as
questions.explanation: An animal that eats other animals is called as carnivore or flesh eater.
questions.explanation_html: An animal that eats other animals is called as carnivore or flesh eater.

questions.question: Which one is not a function of food:
questions.question_html: Which one is not a function of food:
questions.explanation: Food gives us energy, help in growth and repair body parts but do not prepare protein. We obtain protein from plants.
questions.explanation_html: Food gives us energy, help in growth and repair body parts but do not prepare protein. We obtain protein from plants.

questions.question: __________________ is not a milk product.
questions.explanation: Curd, Cheese and Ice-cream are milk products but Pizza is not a milk product.
questions.explanation_html: Curd, Cheese and Ice-cream are milk products but Pizza is not a milk product.

questions.question: Which of the following animals does not have teeth?
questions.question_html: Which of the following animals does not have teeth?
questions.explanation: A mosquito does not has teeth instead it has long, sharp pipe to pierce and suck human wood.
questions.explanation_html: A mosquito does not has teeth instead it has long, sharp pipe to pierce and suck human wood.

questions.question: Snakes do not have
questions.explanation: Snakes​ do not have teeth although they have tongue, mouth and tail. Snake engulf the food without chewing.
questions.explanation_html: Snakes​ do not have teeth although they have tongue, mouth and tail. Snake engulf the food without chewing.

questions.question: Which of the following flowers in edible?
questions.question_html: Which of the following flowers in edible?
questions.explanation: People in some pats of India eat flowers of drum stick, banana and pumpkin.
questions.explanation_html: People in some pats of India eat flowers of drum stick, banana and pumpkin.

questions.question: People of Rajasthan eat roots of khejri trees during
questions.question_html: People of Rajasthan eat roots of khejri trees during
questions.explanation: In Rajasthan when there is insufficient rainfall and crops fail, ie when there is drought, they dug the soil and eat root of khejri trees. These are very nutrition.
questions.explanation_html: In Rajasthan when there is insufficient rainfall and crops fail, ie when there is drought, they dug the soil and eat root of khejri trees. These are very nutrition.

questions.question: What is added to milk to set curd?
questions.question_html: What is added to milk to set curd?
questions.explanation: Bacteria called Lactobacillus is added to set milk into curd.
questions.explanation_html: Bacteria called Lactobacillus is added to set milk into curd.
